content:

Last Friday's eruption at Mount Etna, located on the Italian island of Sicily, created a thick s… [+250 chars]

en: Watch: Mount Etna eruption creates trail of bright orange lava. A stream of thick orange lava was seen trailing from Europe's biggest active volcano, Mount Etna in Italy, snaking through the mountain.

title: Hadean bridgmanite in the source of a present-day ocean island
description: New high-precision neodymium isotope data measured on recently erupted lavas from the submarine Fani Maoré volcano in the Comoros archipelago allow the identification of heterogeneities dating back to Earth’s earliest history.
content:
Geological context of the samples
The Comoros archipelago is the surface manifestation of a deep plume. The main islands that make up this archipelago are Grande Comore, Mohéli and Anjouan, as well … [+29005 chars]
de: Hadeanischer Bridgmanit in der Quelle einer heutigen Ozeaninsel. Neue hochpräzise Neodym-Isotopendaten, die an kürzlich ausgebrochenen Laven des unterseeischen Vulkans Fani Maoré im Komoren-Archipel gemessen wurden, ermöglichen die Identifizierung von Heterogenitäten, die bis in die früheste Erdgeschichte zurückreichen.
en: Hadean bridgmanite in the source of a present-day ocean island. New high-precision neodymium isotope data measured on recently erupted lavas from the submarine Fani Maoré volcano in the Comoros archipelago allow the identification of heterogeneities dating back to Earth’s earliest history.
la: Bridgmanites Hadean in fonte hodiernae insulae oceani. Nova alta praecisio neodymium isotope data mensuratur in lavas subaquaneae Fani Maoré volcano nuper erupit in archipelago Comoros permittit idem heterogeneitatum notarum in prima historia Telluri.

title: A supermassive black hole in a distant galaxy cluster has erupted after approximately 100 million years of complete silence, unleashing a jet of magnetised plasma across nearly a million light-years of space in what the astronomers who observed it have described as a cosmic volcano coming back to life after ages of calm
description: Every large galaxy in the observable universe is thought to host a supermassive black hole at its centre. Most of the time, on the available observational evidence, these black holes are dormant. They accrete only tiny amounts of matter from their immediate s…
content: Every large galaxy in the observable universe is thought to host a supermassive black hole at its centre. Most of the time, on the available observational evidence, these black holes are dormant. The… [+8140 chars]
de: Ein supermassereiches Schwarzes Loch in einem fernen Galaxienhaufen ist nach etwa 100 Millionen Jahren völliger Stille ausgebrochen und hat einen Strahl magnetisierten Plasmas über einen Raum von fast einer Million Lichtjahren freigesetzt. Die Astronomen, die es beobachtet haben, beschreiben es als einen kosmischen Vulkan, der nach Jahren der Ruhe wieder zum Leben erwacht. Es wird angenommen, dass jede große Galaxie im beobachtbaren Universum in ihrem Zentrum ein supermassereiches Schwarzes Loch beherbergt. Den verfügbaren Beobachtungsdaten zufolge sind diese Schwarzen Löcher die meiste Zeit inaktiv. Sie sammeln nur winzige Mengen an Materie aus ihrer unmittelbaren Umgebung an.
en: A supermassive black hole in a distant galaxy cluster has erupted after approximately 100 million years of complete silence, unleashing a jet of magnetised plasma across nearly a million light-years of space in what the astronomers who observed it have described as a cosmic volcano coming back to life after ages of calm. Every large galaxy in the observable universe is thought to host a supermassive black hole at its centre. Most of the time, on the available observational evidence, these black holes are dormant. They accrete only tiny amounts of matter from their immediate s…
la: Foramen supermassivum nigrum in botro galaxia distante erupit postquam circiter 100 miliones annorum perfecti silentii emittens plasma magnetistum per paene decies centena milia spatii per lumen annorum in eo quod astronomi, qui eam observarunt, cosmicum vulcanum post tranquillitatis aetates ad vitam reducentem descripsit. Omnis magna galaxia in universo observabili putatur supermassive foramen nigrum in suo centro continere. Frequentius, in observationibus notatis argumentis, haec nigra foramina sopitant. title: A volcano has erupted remnants of Earth's primordial magma ocean
description: Earth was once covered by a global magma ocean, which later cooled and crystallised – now traces of this primordial event have been found in magma from a young volcano in the Indian Ocean
content:
Submarine reliefs produced by a survey off the coast of Mayotte in 2019, showing the new underwater volcano Fani Maoré
Campagne MAYOBS2
A rising underwater volcano off the coast of Madagascar has b… [+3760 chars]
de: Ein Vulkan hat Überreste des ursprünglichen Magmaozeans der Erde ausgebrochen. Die Erde war einst von einem globalen Magma-Ozean bedeckt, der später abkühlte und kristallisierte – jetzt wurden Spuren dieses Urereignisses im Magma eines jungen Vulkans im Indischen Ozean gefunden
en: A volcano has erupted remnants of Earth's primordial magma ocean. Earth was once covered by a global magma ocean, which later cooled and crystallised – now traces of this primordial event have been found in magma from a young volcano in the Indian Ocean
la: Volcano erupit reliquiae magmae oceani primigeniae Telluris. Terra olim magma oceano globali obtecta est, quae postea refrigerata et crystallina facta est - vestigia huius eventus primordialis in magma a iuvene vulcano in Indico mari inventa sunt.

title: The highest mountain on Earth is not the closest mountain to outer space — because the planet bulges outward at the equator, the summit of a volcano in Ecuador called Chimborazo sits farther from the center of the Earth than Mount Everest does, even though Everest is much taller
description: The specific highest point on the planet Earth, measured by the standard elevation metric of height above mean sea level, is the summit of Mount Everest — the 8,848.86-metre-tall Himalayan peak on the Nepal-Tibet border, first successfully summited by the New…

de: Der höchste Berg der Erde ist nicht der dem Weltraum am nächsten gelegene Berg – da sich der Planet am Äquator nach außen wölbt, liegt der Gipfel eines Vulkans in Ecuador namens Chimborazo weiter vom Erdmittelpunkt entfernt als der Mount Everest, obwohl der Everest viel höher ist. Der spezifisch höchste Punkt auf dem Planeten Erde, gemessen anhand der Standardhöhenmetrik der Höhe über dem mittleren Meeresspiegel, ist der Gipfel des Mount Everest – der 8.848,86 Meter hohe Himalaya-Gipfel an der Grenze zwischen Nepal und Tibet, der erstmals vom Neuen… erfolgreich bestiegen wurde.
